title: Unstoppable Sinner Moves On, Closes In on Masters Milestone, in Rome
description: The Italian kept up his torrid tennis on Monday at the Foro Italico.

title: Sinner crushes Popyrin in Rome to make history with 25-0 Masters 1000 start to the season
description: Jannik Sinner (No 1) brushed past Alexei Popyrin 6-2, 6-0 in 65 minutes. The Italian's Masters 1000 winning streak hits 30 – level with Djokovic for second on the all-time list, one off the Serbian's own record.

title: Jannik Sinner delivers a Rome performance Alexei Popyrin won’t forget
description: Jannik Sinner was trying to make a run that would end in his first Rome Masters title, but how did his third-round match against Alexei Popyrin go?

title: Fight Flight: Gauff Saves Match Point, Fends Off Jovic for Rome Quarterfinal Return
description: Coco Gauff saved a match point at 3-5 in the second set, improving to 22-8 in 2026.

title: Gauff saves match point to reach third straight Rome quarter-final
description: Coco Gauff (No 3) recovered to win 5-7, 7-5, 6-2 against Iva Jovic in Rome. The 17-year-old American had no second serve to lean on – 10 double faults, 33% of points won – and Gauff just had to last her out. Third consecutive Rome quarter-final at 21.

title: Pellegrino completes Italian fairy tale in Rome with stunning Tiafoe upset
description: Andrea Pellegrino (No 155) walked into his first ATP Masters 1000 main draw a week ago and walked out of his Rome third round bound for the round of 16. The Italian qualifier saved two set points in the tie-break before edging Frances Tiafoe (No 17) 7-6(8), 6-1 – and possibly into the path of Jannik Sinner.

title: Rune Withdraws from Hamburg and Roland Garros
description: Holger Rune pauses his comeback plan.

title: Rune withdraws from Hamburg and Roland-Garros as Dane refuses to come back at “less than 110%”
description: Holger Rune is not coming back yet. The 22-year-old Dane, seven months out from Achilles surgery, does not want to feel "almost ready" but "110%" when he returns.

title: Rublev cruises past familiar foe Davidovich Fokina, revenant Basilashvili next
description: Andrey Rublev (No 12) brushed past Alejandro Davidovich Fokina (No 21) 6-4, 6-4 at the Foro Italico to become the first man into the Rome round of 16.

title: May 11th, 1987: The day tennis came back to the Olympics
description: Every day, Tennis Majors takes you back in time to relive a tennis event which happened on this specific day. On May 11th, 1987, the IOC voted to reintroduce tennis at the 1988 Olympics.

title: “I’m trying to be transparent but also not give the whole world my business”: Coco Gauff admits off-court struggles
description: Coco Gauff used her third round Rome press conference to acknowledge months of off-court struggles – without specifying what they are. She plays Iva Jovic in the round of 16 today.

title: Italian Open 2026 Day Five Recap
description: Day Five at the Foro Italico saw the Round of 32 produce some high-quality tennis and a mix of expected results and surprises. Alexander Zverev looked sharp in his best performance of the tournament so far, while qualifier Dino Prizmic continued his run by knocking out another seeded player. On home soil, Lorenzo Musetti advanced …

title: “A little door is always open”: Sorana Cirstea, into the Rome second week, hasn’t quite shut the door on staying
description: Sorana Cirstea was going to retire at the end of 2026. Then, at 36, she beat the world No. 1 and reached the second week of the Italian Open. The door, she now says, is slightly open.

title: “It was like a check”: the rematch Naomi Osaka has been waiting for, and the test Iga Swiatek wants before Paris
description: Two years after their Roland-Garros first-round thriller, Naomi Osaka and Iga Swiatek meet again in Rome – for very different reasons.

title: Clean and Mean: Ruthless Swiatek Finds Her Groove Against Cocciaretto
description: Iga Swiatek defeated Elisabetta Cocciaretto by the exact scoreline as their match in Rome last year - 6-1, 6-0.

title: “There is no one more selfish than a tennis player”: former Roland-Garros head shrugs off the Grand Slam boycott threat
description: Former Roland-Garros tournament director Guy Forget shrugged off the players’ Grand Slam boycott threat in an interview with French website Tennis Actu, dismissing the idea as “bizarre” and questioning the notion that the top players are pushing for higher prize money out of any genuine collective spirit. Forget, who ran the tournament from 2016 to … Continued

title: Casper Ruud Says Sinner May Be Robotic, But It’s Far From an Insult

title: Italian Open 2026 Day Four Recap
description: Day Four at the Foro Italico belonged to the underdogs and the home players. While Jannik Sinner looked every bit the World No. 1 with a comfortable straight-sets win, two significant upsets stole the spotlight: Mariano Navone took out fourth seed Felix Auger-Aliassime in two tight tiebreaks, and qualifier Nikoloz Basilashvili upset fifth seed Ben …

title: Fils retires after 22 minutes in Rome opener against wild-card Pellegrino
description: Arthur Fils (No 15) walked off Court Pietrangeli after 22 minutes and four games, struggling with what appeared to be a physical issue against Italian wild card Andrea Pellegrino (No 155) — the third round goes to the home player without a single completed set. The Frenchman, fresh from a Madrid semi-final last week and projected to meet Jannik Sinner in the round of 16, leaves Rome before he could really arrive.

title: Sinner opens Rome with clinical Ofner win as Masters streak stretches to 29
description: While Aryna Sabalenka, Jasmine Paolini, Félix Auger-Aliassime and Ben Shelton all fell out of Rome on the same afternoon, Jannik Sinner was the pole of stability. The Italian (No 1) eased past Sebastian Ofner 6-3, 6-4 on Campo Centrale to extend his run at Masters 1000 level to 29 matches – and to put one more match between him and the only Masters trophy still missing from his collection.

title: The seed carnage continues in Rome: Basilashvili reaches first Masters 1000 third round in four years, ousting Shelton in three
description: Georgian qualifier Nikoloz Basilashvili, the world No. 117, upset No. 5 seed Ben Shelton 6-4, 6-7(5), 6-3 in the second round of the Rome ATP Masters 1000, claiming his ninth career top-10 win and his first ATP Masters 1000 third round since Indian Wells in 2022. The 34-year-old, a former world No. 16, played with … Continued
